description_long: This is a compact, freestanding public toilet structure with a dark, weathered wooden exterior and a slightly pitched, corrugated metal roof. The front features two vertically aligned doors, each with a rectangular window near the top, and black handles for entry. The sides and back are solid panels with a similar wood texture, lacking any windows or openings. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, providing minimal overhang for rain protection. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible signage or decorative elements, and the structure appears robust and suitable for outdoor urban environments. The windows on the doors likely serve as ventilation or for visibility inside.
description: A compact, dark wooden public toilet with a pitched metal roof, double front doors with small windows, and a sturdy, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing two doors with rectangular windows and black handles, as well as the pitched roof overhang.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a solid wooden panel and the sloped profile of the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a large, uninterrupted wooden panel and the full width of the pitched roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other side with a solid wooden panel and visible roof slope.
attribute_roof_structure: Pitched, gabled roof made of corrugated metal with a slight overhang and no visible skylights or gutters.
